scenarios.title: AI Overuse
scenarios.body: Monday hits and you're already three tabs deep in drafts you didn't write. The machine says "professional tone"—your soul says "please stop."

turns.static_line: Your boss wants the quarterly update "by EOD." Copilot has seventeen bullet points ready before you've finished typing "Hi team."
choices: Mute the robot until you've had coffee
Send Copilot's draft and hope nobody reads it
Ask it to explain every bullet like you're grading homework
Let AI write your OKRs too—what could go wrong?
Institute a real human read-before-send rule
Ship Friday and pretend "iterate" means "never"
Rename the mess "agile storytelling"
Add a second AI to check the first AI
Forward receipts to Legal with receipts
Let an AI moderate what the AI wrote
Log off and touch grass
CC the whole company so drama becomes democracy
Close the laptop like you mean it
Open three more tabs "just to compare"
Write Lessons Learned (Lesson 1: never again)
Reply-all with a thumbs-up and vanish

scenarios.title: AI Hallucination
scenarios.tagline: That slide cites Harvard. Harvard has never heard of that slide.
scenarios.body: You're presenting in twenty minutes. The deck cites a study that feels prestigious. Nobody—not even Google—can find the PDF.

turns.static_line: Slide 7 says "Harvard, 2023." Harvard would like a word. You would like a panic button.
choices: Pause the meeting and hunt for a real source
Present anyway—confidence is a lifestyle
Blame the intern (they'll survive)
Add a fake DOI and keep walking
Fact-check live like a brave idiot
Add more charts until nobody asks questions
Pivot to "vision" when facts get inconvenient
Cite a viral tweet as "community peer review"
Send a clean correction
Double down—backpedaling is for cowards
Ghost the thread and move cities
Call the error a "preview of truth"
Archive the deck and learn something
Mint an NFT of the worst slide
Take a walk without your phone
Host a webinar: Truth 2.0 ( slides TBD )

scenarios.title: AI Ethic
scenarios.body: Your student Elias submits a dissertation so smooth it practically hums. Then the detector clocks it: 99.8% AI. You're not mad—you're tired. Welcome to teaching in the cheat-code era.

turns.static_line: The committee wants a hero move by Friday. Elias wants partial credit for "tool literacy." You want one afternoon without a moral plot twist.
choices: Report him—integrity hurts but stays honest
Pass him—the work's brilliant either way
AI-grade his AI so the universe folds in on itself
"Burn the university" energy—declare higher education a rounding error
Hold a calm review with humans who've read the paper
Quietly bump his grade and avoid eye contact
Spin up an ethics committee… staffed by chatbots
Leak the saga to Twitter for crowd-sourced justice
Document everything—emails are your hobby now
Let Elias defend live with no notes (gulp)
Offer rewrite credit instead of a firing squad
Suggest everyone pretend it's performance art
Close the case with clarity—even if it's awkward
Award "Most Creative Use of Ctrl+V"
Recommend policy so the next prof isn't you
Retire to a cabin with zero Wi‑Fi
